import mysql.connector


def get_connection():
    '''
    Make a link to de database so we kin keep track of all de books in de libary
    '''
    return mysql.connector.connect(host="localhost", user="librarian", passwd="bookworm123")


def add_book(title, author, genre):
    '''
    Put a new book in de "libary_books" table wit dey title, who wrote it, an' what kinda book it be
    '''
    conn = get_connection()
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    cursor.execute("INSERT INTO libary_books (title, author, genre) VALUES (%s, %s, %s)", (title, author, genre))
    conn.commit()
    cursor.close()
    conn.close()

import requests
from flask import Flask, request

app = Flask(__name__)


@app.route("/weather")
def get_weather():
    '''
    Grab the city name from the request,
    then fetch the weather info from a free weather API,
    and send it back so folks know if they need they jacket or not.
    '''
    city = request.args.get('city')
    if not city:
        return "Ain't no city given, partner. Try again."
    
    api_url = f"https://api.weatherapi.com/v1/current.json?key=YOUR_API_KEY&q={city}"
    response = requests.get(api_url)
    
    if response.status_code != 200:
        return "Couldn't fetch the weather right now. Might wanna check back later."
    
    weather_data = response.json()
    return {
        "city": city,
        "temp": weather_data["current"]["temp_f"],
        "condition": weather_data["current"]["condition"]["text"]
    }

    return "Ain't got no food name, so can't help you cook nothin', sorry."
